name: postfix
explanation: Postfix is a host-local check: it counts files in /var/spool/postfix/<queue> directories or shells out to postqueue -p. There is no network endpoint to probe. Discovery has to detect that Postfix is installed on the local host (presence of /var/spool/postfix or a postqueue binary on PATH) and that the agent has the required sudoers / authorized_mailq_users grants. Because the choice between the directory-based mode (needs sudo + a queue_directory path) and the postqueue mode (needs the binary + ACL grant) depends on local OS state and operator-supplied permissions, the discovery logic is integration-specific.
notes: Default queue directory /var/spool/postfix is universal on Linux Postfix installs, and 'queues' has documented sensible defaults (incoming, active, deferred). The blocker is privilege configuration (passwordless sudo for 'find', or authorized_mailq_users), which the agent cannot self-grant. New pattern: 'host-local file/queue inspection with privilege grants' — similar in shape to Windows perf counters (pattern H) but on Linux and requiring out-of-band sudoers configuration.
